commands.review_summary:
The PR adds direct-session origin-label metadata and shortens long direct-chat identifiers in the Control UI session-display fallback helper.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +111, Docs +99. Total +226 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: `ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts` still renders `friendlyKeyLabel ?? row.key` for the primary cell and details title, while the PR edits a helper that this renderer does not call. I did not run a live Control Dashboard session.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The external PR needs code changes plus contributor or maintainer real dashboard proof; do not queue automation while the proof gate is mock-only.

target.title: fix(control-ui): improve session key display for direct chats with long identifiers
target.body:
## Summary

When the Control Dashboard session list displays direct (non-group) sessions without a label or displayName, it falls back to showing the full raw session key — including long identifiers like `ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2` or `agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075e...`. This makes it hard to identify sessions at a glance.

This PR addresses the issue at two levels:

1. **Gateway-side (metadata layer)**: Enrich `displayName` for direct sessions from the origin label during session recording, so the UI has a readable name to display.
2. **UI-side (session-display)**: Truncate long raw identifiers in the fallback display to `prefix_abc...wxyz` instead of showing the full value.

What is intentionally out of scope:

- Resolving `binding.comment` at session recording time (requires config access not available in the message context)
- Changing the table key column's `friendlyKeyLabel` logic (the agent identity display already works well)

## Linked context

Closes #61213

Labels: `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Direct sessions in the Control Dashboard show truncated readable identifiers instead of full raw keys
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout, vitest unit tests
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/session-display.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** All 16 tests pass, including tests that verify long identifiers (34+ chars) are truncated to a short form

```
✓ parseSessionKey > truncates long identifiers in direct session keys (0ms)
✓ parseSessionKey > truncates long user_id identifiers (0ms)
✓ parseSessionKey > preserves short identifiers as-is (0ms)
```

- **What was not tested:** Live Control Dashboard rendering (the changes are in pure functions used during session recording and session key display)
- **Proof limitations:** Unit tests use mock session data; dashboard rendering verification requires a real gateway session list

## Tests and validation

- `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/session-display.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check`

What regression coverage was added or updated:

- 16 new tests in `ui/src/ui/session-display.test.ts` covering `parseSessionKey()` and `resolveSessionDisplayName()` with various session key formats and identifier lengths
- Existing session formats (main, subagent, cron, group) are confirmed unchanged
- Direct sessions with identifiers >20 chars now truncate to `abc...wxyz` format

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — direct session fallback names are truncated for readability. Label/displayName-aware sessions are unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.

What is the highest-risk area? The metadata change adds `displayName` origin derivation for direct sessions; existing group displayName behavior is unaffected.

## Current review state

Next action: Maintainer / ClawSweeper review
